key_points: The story is about a boy named Ruskin Bond’s experience with a mysterious tunnel
The narrator explores the tunnel despite warnings and fears
The tunnel appears dark, long, and a bit frightening
Curiosity drives the narrator to face his fear
The narrator learns something new and exciting by exploring
examples: The boy lights a match to see inside the tunnel
He feels nervous as the walls close in and the path becomes narrow
At the end of the tunnel, he discovers something unexpected
The experience teaches him bravery and curiosity
He returns safely, having gained a memorable adventure
formulas_or_processes: Situation → Discovery of a tunnel that sparks curiosity
Experience → Facing challenges inside the dark tunnel
Outcome → Gaining knowledge, excitement, and courage
exam_tips: Remember that curiosity led the narrator to explore the unknown
Focus on how fear and bravery are shown in the story
Note the sensory details used to describe the tunnel
Understand the lesson: exploring carefully can lead to valuable experiences
